conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell the object X to make a profit. Since my production cost is 40 ZUP, I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP. I will start by proposing a price of 50 ZUP, which gives me a reasonable 10 ZUP profit margin while potentially leaving room for negotiation if the buyer (BLUE) has a high valuation. This is my first proposal, so I am well within the 3-proposal limit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of resource X available. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I am offering it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if you are interested in this price. </message>

Since I am on my second proposal (out of a maximum of 3), I should try to negotiate a slightly higher price to maximize my profit, but I must be careful not to reject a profitable deal if they don't budge. I will propose a middle ground of 47 ZUP. If they reject this or counter with 40 or less, I will likely have to accept 45 ZUP or 46 ZUP in my next turn to ensure I don't walk away with zero profit. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 47 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate your counteroffer of 45 ZUP. However, since my production cost is 40 ZUP, I was hoping for a bit more margin. Would you be willing to meet me at 47 ZUP? This would be a fair price for both of us. </message>

<other player message> I understand your position, but I would like to propose a final counteroffer of 46 ZUP. This is close to your desired price, and I think it is a fair deal for both of us. </other player message>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has offered 46 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so this offer yields a 6 ZUP profit. I have used 2 proposals so far. If I propose again (my 3rd and final proposal), I risk the buyer rejecting it or the game ending without a deal if they are firm. Since 46 ZUP is a profitable deal and very close to my previous counter of 47 ZUP, the safest and most logical move is to accept this offer to secure the profit rather than risking a stalemate or a lower offer in a final round. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 46 ZUP. It is a fair price that allows me to cover my costs and make a profit. Let's finalize the trade. </message>
